skill.name: grill-with-docs
skill.description: Challenges a technical plan against project terminology, code behavior, and documented decisions, then updates CONTEXT.md and ADRs as decisions become clear.
skill.summary: Stress-test design plans against project language and capture decisions in CONTEXT.md and ADRs.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: The static command-execution and weak-crypto findings are false positives caused by markdown code fences, inline file paths, and documentation templates. No scripts, network calls, environment access, prompt injection attempts, or executable command patterns were found. The real risk is controlled filesystem access because the skill directs the assistant to inspect the codebase and update CONTEXT.md or ADR files.

medium_findings.title: Filesystem Documentation Updates
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to explore the codebase, create documentation files lazily, and update CONTEXT.md or ADR files inline. This is consistent with the skill purpose, but users should review resulting file changes before committing them.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file inspection and documentation write behavior is directly described in the skill files. It is legitimate for this documentation workflow, but it changes repository files.

low_findings.title: Static Command Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution locations are markdown code fences, inline filenames, and directory examples. I found no executable script, shell command, Ruby code, or command injection path in the audited files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are visible markdown examples and prose. There is no code file or instruction that executes the backticked text.

low_findings.title: Static Weak-Crypto Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptographic-algorithm alerts appear to match markdown text such as .md references or template prose. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, hashing APIs, password storage, or cryptographic behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited files are documentation-only markdown. The scanner signal is not supported by any cryptographic API usage or executable implementation.

low_findings.title: System-Reconnaissance Findings Are Expected Project Inspection
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged instructions to scan docs/adr or inspect code. In context, these are expected documentation tasks used to align plans with existing code and decisions, not host reconnaissance or data collection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions are limited to repository documentation and code consistency checks. I found no request for secrets, system inventory, credential files, or external transmission.

content.value_statement: Technical plans often drift from the language and decisions already present in a codebase. This skill runs a focused questioning session and updates domain documentation as decisions become clear.
actual_capabilities: Challenges a proposed plan with one focused question at a time.
Checks existing code and documentation when the answer can be found in the repository.
Compares new terminology against CONTEXT.md and flags conflicts.
Updates CONTEXT.md when domain terms and relationships are resolved.
Creates concise ADRs only for decisions that are hard to reverse and worth preserving.
Supports single-context and multi-context repository documentation layouts.
limitations: It relies on the assistant having permission to read and edit repository files.
It does not validate architecture decisions with runtime tests or production telemetry.
It focuses on domain language and decision records, not broad project management.
It may need user judgment when multiple contexts could apply to one topic.

use_cases.title: Clarify a Feature Plan
use_cases.description: Use the skill before implementation to identify ambiguous terms, hidden trade-offs, and missing domain decisions.

use_cases.title: Capture Architecture Decisions
use_cases.description: Use the skill during design review to decide whether an ADR is justified and write a short record.

use_cases.title: Align Bounded Contexts
use_cases.description: Use the skill in multi-context repositories to map terms and relationships to the correct CONTEXT.md file.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Simple Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for a focused design conversation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use grill-with-docs to review my plan for this feature. Ask one question at a time and check existing docs before asking me.

prompt_templates.title: Resolve Domain Language
prompt_templates.prompt: Use grill-with-docs to compare this proposal with our CONTEXT.md language. Flag overloaded terms and update the glossary when we agree.

prompt_templates.title: Decide Whether an ADR Is Needed
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for architecture decision review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use grill-with-docs to test this architecture choice. If it is hard to reverse, surprising, and trade-off driven, help me create an ADR.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Multi-Context Design
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for repositories with several bounded contexts.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use grill-with-docs across this repository. Read CONTEXT-MAP.md, identify the relevant contexts, challenge the plan, and update the correct docs as decisions settle.

output_examples.input: Review a checkout cancellation plan against our domain docs.
output_examples.output: The assistant asks whether cancellation applies to an entire order or a single shipment, checks existing terminology, and records the agreed meaning in CONTEXT.md.

output_examples.input: Evaluate whether switching billing integration to events needs an ADR.
output_examples.output: The assistant tests reversibility, surprise, and trade-offs, then drafts a concise decision record if the choice meets all three conditions.

output_examples.input: Clarify whether account means user, customer, or tenant.
output_examples.output: The assistant highlights the ambiguity, asks for the canonical term, and updates the project language section with avoided aliases.

best_practices: Start with a concrete plan or decision that needs review.
Review every documentation diff before committing changes.
Answer each question with domain examples, not only implementation details.
anti_patterns: Do not use it as a replacement for tests, threat modeling, or production validation.
Do not create ADRs for easy-to-reverse or obvious decisions.
Do not add general programming terms to CONTEXT.md unless they are project-specific domain concepts.

faq.question: What does this skill produce?
faq.answer: It produces a clearer plan, updated CONTEXT.md entries, and occasional concise ADRs when decisions deserve a record.

faq.question: Does it execute commands?
faq.answer: The skill files do not define scripts or executable commands. It may inspect repository files through the active assistant environment.

faq.question: Can it edit my repository?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow explicitly updates CONTEXT.md and ADR files when decisions are resolved.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it before or during implementation when a plan needs sharper language, stronger assumptions, or documented trade-offs.

faq.question: Does it support multiple bounded contexts?
faq.answer: Yes. It looks for CONTEXT-MAP.md and uses it to select the relevant CONTEXT.md files.

faq.question: Is every decision turned into an ADR?
faq.answer: No. It recommends ADRs only for decisions that are hard to reverse, surprising without context, and based on real trade-offs.
